mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, the labor market tightens sufficiently to trigger wage growth and consumer spending acceleration, which directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE closes below 5.0%, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and industrial production confirm broad-based demand expansion across both consumer durables and capital goods sectors, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate revenue growth accelerates.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero directional accuracy below 52% over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or forward returns do not statistically differ from the unconditional mean (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, rising industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which historically precedes equity market rallies as earnings revisions turn positive over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of post-recession episodes, or the signal's information coefficient remains statistically indistinguishable from zero across all recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation readings paired with sub-3% federal funds rates signal a policy accommodation mismatch that markets price as imminent tightening, compressing forward valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined inflation-rate signal yields positive 20-day forward returns in over 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales figures directly reflect accelerating consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive short-term equity returns over a 5-day horizon as analysts update near-term earnings forecasts upward.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth fails to correlate with positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion regimes, months where nonfarm payrolls exceed 150,000 while the unemployment rate remains stable confirm robust labor demand that sustains consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon before monetary tightening impacts valuations.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of late-cycle months, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes (partial correlation < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth accelerates above 3.0 percent on a quarterly basis, it signals strong economic momentum that historically drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon as corporate profit margins expand and investor risk appetite increases.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 3.0% coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the signal's directional accuracy does not exceed the baseline win rate of 65.5% over a 10-year walk-forward test.
